frameworks.label: Next.js
frameworks.pitch: Next.js on Workers is powered by the OpenNext adapter (@opennextjs/cloudflare), which converts the Next.js output into a format that runs natively on the Cloudflare edge — no Node.js runtime required.

frameworks.label: Astro
frameworks.pitch: Astro supports both SSR and hybrid (partial prerender) output modes on Cloudflare Workers via @astrojs/cloudflare, letting you mix static islands with server-rendered routes in a single deploy.

frameworks.label: React Router
frameworks.pitch: React Router 7 ships a first-class framework mode that pairs with @cloudflare/vite-plugin, giving you file-based routing, loaders, and actions all running at the Cloudflare edge.

frameworks.label: Nuxt
frameworks.pitch: Nuxt uses its built-in Nitro engine with the cloudflare-module preset, so you get Nuxt's full-stack DX — server routes, composables, auto-imports — compiled directly to a Cloudflare Worker.

frameworks.label: Hono
frameworks.pitch: Hono is designed from the ground up for the WinterCG runtime and runs natively on Cloudflare Workers without any adapter layer, giving you the smallest possible cold-start overhead.

frameworks.label: SvelteKit
frameworks.pitch: SvelteKit integrates with Cloudflare Workers through @sveltejs/adapter-cloudflare, which compiles your server routes and load functions to a Worker that binds directly to D1, KV, R2, and Queues.
